summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a stellar 1991 Garden show featuring smoking Half‑Step, epic Comes a Time and soaring Attics.
review: The recording is an excellent sound‑board source – clean, crisp and virtually free of drop‑outs – that lets the band’s tight, high‑energy performance shine.  Highlights include a thunderous Half‑Step opener, multiple superb renditions of Comes a Time, Attics and Bertha, plus inspired playing from Jerry, Bruce and Vince.  A few reviewers note an over‑aggressive Hornsby touch on the second set, but overall the show is celebrated as one of the best of the ’90s.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is 4.7/5; reviewers overwhelmingly praise both performance and audio quality, with only one mixed comment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with minimal drop‑outs; some reviews note a simultaneous audience feed but the soundboard dominates and is described as electrifying.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans rave about seamless transitions – Half‑Step kicking off set 2, a flowing sequence through Comes a Time → Uncle John’s → The Music Never Stopped → Attics – with both sets maintaining high intensity.  Only the second‑set keyboard dominance is mentioned as a minor blemish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Dominates the night with solid vocals and soaring lead guitar; solos on Bertha, Comes a Time and other jams are highlighted as among his best of the era.
band_member_comments.Bob: Delivers strong rhythm work and memorable licks, especially on Minglewood Blues and early Bertha.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (and occasional Bruce Hornsby) provide lush, sometimes over‑aggressive keyboard textures; praised for dazzling intro on the second set but critiqued for overwhelming some tunes.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming described as tight throughout and especially powerful post‑drum sections leading into the encore.
